definition: A step-by-step plan computers follow so they can look smarter than us in meetings.
A mobile operating system that proves robots can live inside your pocket.
The invisible internet pipe that decides whether your video call becomes a slideshow.
A digital ledger that explains itself so seriously everyone nods and pretends to understand.
The app you open to ask the internet questions you refuse to ask humans.
Stored computer memory that acts helpful until it stubbornly shows yesterday’s website.
A program that translates your code while silently judging every semicolon.
A tiny browser file that remembers you, unlike your favorite coffee shop barista.
The digital art of locking doors hackers keep trying to open with crowbars made of code.
The process of turning information into secret spaghetti only the right key can untangle.
A cable connection that makes your internet feel like it finally drank coffee.
A digital bouncer that keeps shady internet guests out of your computer club.
A billion bytes of storage, which is somehow still not enough for your photos.
A digital home address for your device, minus the nosy neighbors and garden gnomes.
A programming language that runs on everything—from your toaster to your spaceship, probably.
A programming language that makes websites dance and developers whisper at consoles.
A gaming device that tests the limits of your thumbs and your patience.
A letter jungle where your fingers roam in search of typos and accidental emojis.
A thousand bytes—enough memory to store your top-secret text file from the ‘90s.
A portable computer that loves your lap but hates your battery life.
A focused beam of light that can cut steel, zap cats, or dazzle dance floors.
An operating system loved by servers, penguins, and people who enjoy terminals.
Teaching computers to learn, so they can outsmart us and take our snack breaks.
A million bytes of data—enough space for memes, but not all your cat photos.
A tiny electronic brain that runs gadgets while pretending size does not matter.
A small box that brings internet home and occasionally demands a dramatic restart.
One billionth of a second, or the exact time your alarm waits to ring again.
A cosmic cloud of dust or a code name for futuristic projects—take your pick.
The end of the Greek alphabet—perfect for naming your final, unstoppable code project.
A light particle so fast, it leaves your shadow feeling out of shape.
A tiny square of screen color that joins millions of friends to make pictures.
A temperamental device that loves paper jams more than printing your final report.
A set of digital manners that computers follow more politely than humans ever could.
A four-rotor drone that buzzes around like a caffeinated mechanical mosquito.
When your computer decides it can be two places at once, blowing your mind.
The keyboard layout that ensures your fingers dance an awkward waltz for typos.
A detection system that says ‘Peek-a-boo!’ to planes, ships, and speeding drivers.
A small box that distributes Wi-Fi and accepts blame for everything online.
A computer that serves data and quietly wonders why everyone keeps asking for more.
Invisible computer instructions that behave perfectly until demo day.
A tiny electronic switch that helped computers shrink from rooms into pockets.
Sending a file upward into the internet cloud and hoping it behaves.
